name: Tier change success (by MemberId)
description: Documented sample success response for the Update Member Tier (TierProcessing) process. Member upgraded from Silver to Gold. Body is the Loyalty Process Output object. Request example uses MemberId.
notes:
IMPLEMENTER-DEFINED: The doc states explicitly (line ~23339) that "The request and response parameters vary depending on the input and output parameters configured for the TierProcessing process type in a Salesforce org." So the processParameters input fields (MemberId / MembershipNumber / NewTier / ReasonForChange) and the outputParameters.results fields (CurrentTier / TierExpirationDate / PreviousTier / EffectiveDate) come from the documented sample but are NOT guaranteed for every org — they reflect the default out-of-the-box TierProcessing template.

PATH: The actual process name in the path is TierProcessing (per the resource example table at line 22592, group=Members). NOTE the doc's prose Example URL at line ~23323 shows the path ending in "updateTier" (a sample process name for that org's configured process), while the resource table lists the template/process API name as TierProcessing. I used TierProcessing in sfPath to match the target. Caller substitutes {programName} (doc uses DemoProgram).

WRAPPER: processParameters is the standard required request wrapper (type: Loyalty Process Parameter Map). Response body schema name: Loyalty Process Output (top-level keys: message, outputParameters, simulationDetails, status — all confirmed from the documented JSON example).

VERSIONS: Loyalty Program Process resource Available version = 56.0; TierProcessing value available in API version 56.0+ (line 8018). Resource Example URLs in the doc use v67.0 (Summer 26). MembershipNumber/MemberId are interchangeable request fields (doc note references "Loyalty Management Connect API Interchangeable Request Fields").

No documented error/empty fixture: the doc shows only success-shaped responses (status:true) for this process, so I did not fabricate an error fixture. An org error would surface via the standard Loyalty Process Output with status:false and a populated message string, but no concrete error example is in the doc.
